key_insight: A candidate phase may be thermodynamically stable on the convex hull yet still be unsynthesizable if the interfacial melt at that composition is unstable to spinodal decomposition.
engineering_relevance: This adds an atomistic, melt-stability screen to AI materials discovery, helping prioritize phases that are not only stable on paper but also realistically synthesizable.
method: Melt-quench molecular dynamics on Fe--B using a fine-tuned machine-learning interatomic potential
Assess local melt stability via free-energy curvature and concentration--concentration structure factor
Compare ambient-pressure and applied-pressure behavior near the FeB4 composition
evidence_signal: In Fe--B, the B-rich melt near FeB4 becomes concave and demixes at ambient pressure, while pressure adds a convex PV term that stabilizes the melt and matches the experimental synthesis boundary.
watch_item: The descriptor depends on reliable interatomic potentials and composition-specific atomistic simulations, so accuracy may be limited by model transferability and computational cost.
paper_title: Interfacial-melt stability as a thermodynamic prerequisite for solid-state synthesis
